Look up the label for a single variable

Source: R/label_map.R
get_label.Rd

Returns the descriptive label for one variable name from a label map. This is a safer alternative to the manual match() pattern, providing clear errors on typos and missing variables.

Usage

get_label(label_map_df, variable)

Arguments

label_map_df

A data frame with key and label columns, as returned by label_map.

variable

A single character string: the variable name to look up.

Value

A single character string: the label for the requested variable.

Examples

dta <- generate_survival_data(n = 50, seed = 42)
lmap <- label_map(dta)

get_label(lmap, "age")
#> [1] "Age at surgery (years)"
get_label(lmap, "hgb_bs")
#> [1] "Baseline hemoglobin (g/dL)"

# Use in plot titles
var <- "lvefvs_b"
plot(dta[[var]], main = get_label(lmap, var), ylab = get_label(lmap, var))

On this page
